predictions.text: Fertilo-style egg-maturation-outside-the-body technology will change IVF, making it cheaper, faster, and less painful.
Artificial wombs will eventually make traditional childbirth optional, 'like riding horses for transport.'
AI will replace human co-founders/employees for many operational tasks (marketing copy, conversion optimization, messaging) within a few years.
AI agents will soon generate ideas as well as execute them, potentially devaluing the 'idea person' advantage smart people currently have.

dynamics: Two-host, no-guest discussion episode structured around two long segments: (1) Sam recounts a Nassim Taleb-inspired 'crystal ball' investing experiment, and (2) both riff extensively on how they've started using ChatGPT as a daily life/business assistant. Energy is exploratory and enthusiastic, especially in the AI segment, with Shaan attempting to explain deep learning/LLMs from scratch. Closes with an existential tangent about AI eventually replacing even 'idea people,' ending on a comedic note (an outro song, 'Another White Boy With a Podcast').
frameworks: Crystal ball test: even given tomorrow's news 24 hours early, most traders still fail because bet-sizing/leverage discipline matters more than predictive accuracy
Ask uncommonly clear questions (Tim Ferriss): confusion comes from vague questions; clarity comes from precise ones — applies to prompting AI and to yourself
What are the dogs not barking: look for the absence of expected signals (complaints, problems) as a diagnostic tool, borrowed from Amazon's OP1 writing culture and Sherlock Holmes
Why am I not already a billionaire?: instead of asking how to reach a goal, ask why it isn't already true — surfaces concrete knowledge or execution gaps rather than abstract ambition
learnings: Having advance access to news doesn't reliably improve trading outcomes because most people mis-size their bets even when directionally correct; sizing discipline matters more than information itself.

Even professional traders in the crystal-ball experiment were only marginally more accurate (57% vs. 51%) than amateurs, but vastly outperformed (130% vs. 3% average gain) because of disciplined position sizing.
Using an AI assistant as a question-asker (having it interview you) rather than an answer-giver produces a more effective 'thought partner' experience than direct Q&A.
Organizing AI usage into persistent 'project' folders (health, wardrobe, life-coaching) with uploaded personal documents creates much more contextually useful responses than one-off prompts.
A press release's claimed breakthrough (like Fertilo's out-of-body egg maturation) should be interrogated for spin, sample size, and peer review before being treated as proven.

banger_quotes.quote: He who lives by the crystal ball will die eating shattered glass.
Ask a better question, get a better answer.
What are we stupid for not doing right now?
It's kind of like you have Neuralink, they just never did the surgery.

numbers_dropped: In the crystal-ball experiment, half of 118 finance-trained participants lost money and 1 in 6 lost everything despite having next-day news
The professional trader subgroup (5 people) averaged 130% gains vs. 3.2% for the casual group
Participants were directionally correct only 51% of the time (amateurs) vs. 57% (pros) despite having the news
A hacking group that stole press releases in advance made hundreds of millions of dollars before being caught
Sam's company's stock rose from ~$350 to ~$460 after an acquisition was mentioned on an earnings call (Feb 2021)
Cathie Wood's ARK reportedly underperformed index funds over ~15 years while earning ~$100 million a year in fees
notable: Despite the title referencing Nassim Taleb, he is not a guest — the hosts discuss his ideas and a related academic experiment (the 'crystal ball test') as a pure two-host segment, followed by an extended personal show-and-tell of how each uses ChatGPT day to day.
